title: Reported speech
explanation:
Reported speech (statements)

When we report what someone said, we often change the tense back:

  • Present → past: "I am tired" → She said (that) she was tired.
  • Past → past perfect: "I saw him" → He said he had seen him.
  • Will → would: "I will help" → She said she would help.

Time words change too: today → that day, tomorrow → the next day.

questions.text: "I am busy." → She said she [*was|is|am] busy.
"I like chocolate." → He said he [*liked|likes|like] chocolate.
"I will call you." → She said she [*would call|will call|calls] me.
"We live in Bristol." → They said they [*lived|live|living] in Bristol.
"I can't swim." → He said he [*couldn't|can't|could not to] swim.
"I saw the film." → She said she [*had seen|saw|sees] the film.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: "I'm tired," he said. → He said he ____ tired. | *was | is | am
"We are leaving," they said. → They said they ____. | *were leaving | are leaving | leave
"I don't know," she said. → She said she ____. | *didn't know | doesn't know | not know
"I have finished," he said. → He said he ____ finished. | *had | has | have
"I went home early." → She said she [*had gone|went|goes] home early.
"I'll help you." → He said he [*would help|will help|helps] me.
"She is at work." → He said she [*was|is|be] at work.
"We don't want to go." → They said they [*didn't want|don't want|not want] to go.
"I can speak three languages." → She said she [*could speak|can speak|speaks] three languages.
